mission.title: consul: Support HTTP connection protocol upgrades thru Envoy
mission.description: Support HTTP connection protocol upgrades thru Envoy. This issue affects 44+ users.

steps.title: Identify consul upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your consul deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
#### Feature Description

HTTP supports a [protocol upgrade mechanism](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Protocol_upgrade_mechanism) which allows upgrading existing connections to a different protocol such as WebSocket.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support HTTP connection protocol upgrades thru Envoy
steps.description:
This should solve #8283 

Credit goes to  , I simply rebased his commit on master with small changes to get CI passing to get this PR considered for merging.
```yaml
curl 'http://dashboard.ingress.consul/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=websocket' \
  --include \
  --header 'Origin: http://dashboard.ingress.consul' \
  --header 'Connection: keep-alive, Upgrade' \
  --header 'Upgrade: websocket' \
  --header 'Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13' \
  --header 'Sec-WebSocket-Key: O1b2cWbnz4bk8LyYKDiE3Q=='

HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
date: Wed, 08 Jul 2020 06:29:45 GMT
server: envoy
connection: close
content-length: 0
```

steps.title: Confirm Support HTTP connection protocol upgrades thru… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

This should solve #8283 

Credit goes to  , I simply rebased his commit on master with small changes to get CI passing to get this PR considered for merging.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
